topic: Shapley value attribution for risk factors
user_query: PAF calculations can double-count when risk factors overlap. How should we handle this?
ai_response_summary: Explained two approaches: (1) Joint PAF formula 1-∏(1-PAF_k) which assumes independence but can underestimate joint effects, (2) Shapley value decomposition from cooperative game theory, which fairly allocates the total burden among risk factors by averaging marginal contributions across all possible coalitions. The Shapley approach satisfies efficiency (allocations sum to total), symmetry (equal contributors get equal credit), and null player (zero-contribution factors get zero). Computational cost is O(2^n) for n risk factors but tractable for ~10 factors.
